name: Cyprus
contextBlurb: Cyprus applies the EU’s MiCA framework to stablecoins. ART/EMT rules have applied since 30 Jun 2024, with the rest of MiCA in force since 30 Dec 2024; pre-existing CASPs may operate under the Art. 143(3) transitional regime until 1 Jul 2026. CySEC is the national competent authority for CASPs and MiCA Title II (and parts of Titles III/V/VI), while the Central Bank of Cyprus (CBC) is the competent authority for Title IV (EMTs) and handles e-money/payment-institution supervision. Stablecoins are categorized as EMTs (fiat-referenced) or ARTs (asset-referenced), with issuer obligations on reserves, disclosures, and redemption set directly by MiCA and Level-2 measures.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
CySEC Circular C722 flags EBA/ESMA guidance on the PSD2/3 ↔ MiCA interplay, clarifying payment-services implications for CASPs.
Cyprus publishes Law 96(I)/2025 (AML Law Amendment No. 2) in the Official Gazette, strengthening AML/CFT rules applicable to CASPs and transfer-of-funds obligations.
The Central Bank of Cyprus’ new AML/CFT Directive 2025 enters into force (published 2 May 2025), updating requirements for supervised institutions including EMIs/PSPs.
Remaining MiCA provisions become applicable EU-wide (beyond ART/EMT), with transitional arrangements for CASPs.
MiCA Titles III–IV on ARTs/EMTs (stablecoins) apply across the EU, introducing reserve, redemption and governance rules.

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Cyprus applies the EU’s MiCA framework to stablecoins. ART/EMT rules have applied since 30 Jun 2024, with the rest of MiCA in force since 30 Dec 2024; pre-existing CASPs may operate under the Art. 143(3) transitional regime until 1 Jul 2026. CySEC is the national competent authority for CASPs and MiCA Title II (and parts of Titles III/V/VI), while the Central Bank of Cyprus (CBC) is the competent authority for Title IV (EMTs) and handles e-money/payment-institution supervision. Stablecoins are categorized as EMTs (fiat-referenced) or ARTs (asset-referenced), with issuer obligations on reserves, disclosures, and redemption set directly by MiCA and Level-2 measures.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Stablecoins in Cyprus are classified under the EU MiCA regime as e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs). EMTs reference a single official currency and must be redeemable at par in funds; only credit institutions or electronic-money institutions may issue them and CBC is the compete
